title: Xenophobia
content:
{{Top|legends=Speciesism}} {{Update|[[Queen's Hope]], {{HelmetCollectionCite|11|Helmets}}, [[Thrawn Ascendancy: Chaos Rising]], [[The Sand Will Provide]], [[The Veteran]], [[Shadow Fall: An Alphabet Squadron Novel|Shadow Fall]], [[Shadow of the Sith]], [[Join the Resistance]], [[Galaxy's Edge: Black Spire]], [[Dawn of Rebellion]], [[Star Wars: The Rise and Fall of the Galactic Empire]]}} {{Quote|…I have no issues with aliens myself—some of my best friends are nonhumans—but some grav-ball fans object to alien physiognomies, seeing them as giving nonhumans an edge.|Janus Fhurek's xenophobic rationale for kicking Frid Kelio and Hench Sina off the AppSci SaberCats|''[[Servants of the Empire: Edge of the Galaxy]]''}} [[File:Xenophobic_Sephi.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A group of xenophobic Sephi assaulting the human [[Jun Sato]]]] '''Xenophobia''', also known as '''speciesism''',''[[Star Wars Propaganda: A History of Persuasive Art in the Galaxy]]'' or '''anti-alien bias''',{{HelmetCollectionCite|70|Weapons & Uniforms}} was dislike or prejudice toward individuals from other species or cultures. [[Zare Leonis]] regarded his former [[athletic director]] [[Janus Fhurek]] as xenophobic for excluding his two [[alien]] classmates [[Frid Kelio]] and [[Hench Sina]] from the [[Junior Academy of Applied Sciences]]'s [[grav-ball]] team [[AppSci SaberCats]].''[[Servants of the Empire: Imperial Justice]]'' Fhurek had objected to Kelio and Sina's presence on the AppSci SaberCats on the grounds that their alien physiognomies gave them an unfair advantage. Leonis' experiences with Fhurek led him to believe that the [[Galactic Empire]] condoned prejudice toward individuals belonging to the "wrong species."''[[Servants of the Empire: The Secret Academy]]'' By the time of the [[Battle of Endor]], it was widely known that the Empire pursued a xenophobic policy that favored [[human]]s and treated aliens as subjects or threats.''[[Aftermath: Life Debt]]'' ==History== ===Clone Wars=== {{Quote|I don't trust anyone who isn't a Pyke.|Marg Krim, to [[Rafa Martez]]|{{TCW|Deal No Deal}}}} During the [[Clone Wars]], the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]] consisted of several different [[alien]] groups. According to [[Ensign]] [[Eli N. Vanto|Eli Vanto]], this combined with the devastation of the war created a lot of resentment among [[human]]s. While several alien species were allied with the [[Galactic Republic]], there was still a prevailing perception that humans still carried most of the weight of the war effort. This sentiment carried on into the [[Imperial Era|Age of the Empire]]. [[Core Worlds]] humans were known to look down on both humans and nonhumans from beyond the [[Mid Rim Territories|Mid Rim]], particularly people from [[Wild Space]] and the [[Unknown Regions]].[[Thrawn (novel)|''Thrawn'']] Even before the war, some resented the [[Neimoidian]]s for being part of the [[Trade Federation]], including [[Senator]] [[Padmé Amidala]], whose [[homeworld]] [[Naboo]] was [[Battle of Naboo|invaded]] by the Neimoidian [[Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray]], though Amidala knew she shouldn't hate an entire species because of the actions of a few of its members.''[[Queen's Shadow]]'' [[Marg Krim]], the [[crime lord]] of the [[Pyke Syndicate]] towards the [[Mission to Mustafar|end]] of the Clone Wars, openly distrusted any individual who was not a member of [[Pyke|his species]]. ===Galactic Empire=== ====Humanocentrism==== {{Main|High Human}} {{Quote|Well, officially we're not allowed to disrespect aliens, either. I say officially, because that what's the General Orders say we're supposed to do. But that's not always what we really do.''"
"''You dislike nonhumans?|Eli Vanto and Thrawn discussing xenophobia in the Empire|}} [[File:ImperialXenophobe-Thrawn1.png|thumb|left|250px|Thrawn had to deal with anti-alien xenophobia in the Empire]] The [[Galactic Empire]] was known to favor humans while looking down on non-humans or "aliens".''[[Star Wars Rebels: The Visual Guide]]'' As a result, the Empire's government and [[Imperial Military|military]] was largely dominated by humans, and it was rare to find nonhumans serving as [[Imperial officer]]s.''[[Star Wars Rebels: Visual Guide: Epic Battles]]'' Even in systems where humans were comparatively rare, they were still more frequently promoted to positions of authority over other species.''[[Dawn of Rebellion]]'' While Imperial [[General Orders]] instructed Imperial military personnel not to disrespect aliens, this was often ignored. The [[Imperial Navy]] and [[Imperial High Command]] also commonly ignored the [[Imperial Senate]]'s [[Unknown Alien protocols]]. The [[Imperial Academy|Imperial Academies]] were dominated by humans, and aliens like [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo|Thrawn]] found themselves having to navigate racial prejudice and outright hostility. Imperial justice system was strongly biased against other species. Imperial soldiers were quick to target other species for investigation, even for minor crimes, and were more willing to use lethal force. Not all Imperials disrespected aliens, however; the alien [[Freck]] from [[Mapuzo]] got along with three stormtroopers, whom he notably ferried at least once.{{Kenobi|Part III}} Anyway, the Empire's usual cruel treatment of aliens often forced them to either flee to the [[Outer Rim Territories]] or join the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic]] to counter.''[[Star Wars: The Visual Encyclopedia]]'' Aliens who joined rebel groups to resist the Empire were exploited in propaganda, reinforcing stereotypes and deepening discrimination. This single-species domination was an intentional part of Imperial policy. Imperial propaganda actively supported state-sponsored ethnocentric xenophobia and fear-mongering. To the Empire, "aliens" were by and large unwelcome within its order, with aliens being seen as "different" and "strange". As such, the Empire treated nonhuman segments of its populations as serfs, slaves, or obstacles needing to be tamed, removed, or ignored. The Empire supported the idea that aliens were untrustworthy—unlike humans—and behind their veneer of innocence lurked vicious monsters. The Imperial propaganda machine was so effective in its message that even [[near-human]] species exhibited an inherent distrust toward nonhuman populations. Some people tried to clear and absolve themselves from suspicion of humanocentrism by claiming to have friends of a targeted demographic, all while expressing prejudice in their actions. The Empire implemented the "[[High Human]]" culture prevalently on [[Coruscant]], their capital world, suggesting the superiority of the human race.''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]'' One of the [[Emperor's adviser]]s, [[Janus Greejatus]], hated the idea of living among aliens whatsoever and hoped to see non-humans removed from the Core Worlds and [[Colonies]] regions, advocating to his fellow advisors that they be housed in reservations established within the Rim [[star system]]s. To Greejatus, it was a logical idea. His fellow advisor, [[Pebimarus Xorn]], instead argued aliens needed to be seen as a resource for the Empire's economy and used for their labor. Imperial Governor [[Vashin]] had thoughts on "species compatibility," a phrase understood by [[senator]] [[Mon Mothma]] to be an euphemism used by human supremacists.''[[Reign of the Empire: The Mask of Fear]]'' ====Effects on aliens==== {{Quote|My Great Grand-Nikto told me about the Empire. There were stories of the stormtroopers not being very nice to our people.|[[Neeku Vozo]]|{{Resistance|The First Order Occupation}}}} [[File:BodachiKerev.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Bodach'i slaves on Kerev Doi]] Nonhuman species like [[Wookiee]]s, [[Mon Calamari]],{{Databank|mon-calamari|Mon Calamari}} the [[Geonosian]]s,''[[Catalyst: A Rogue One Novel]]'' the [[Petrusian]]s,[[Lando - Double or Nothing 1|''Lando - Double or Nothing'' 1]] and the [[Bodach'i]]''[[Lost Stars]]'' were known to be exploited as [[Slavery|slave]] labor by the Empire. The Empire repealed laws banning slavery and reclassified several species, particularly Wookiees, as non-[[Sentience|sentient]]. Other species such as the Geonosians,[[Darth Vader (2015) 4|''Darth Vader'' (2015) 4]] the [[Lasat]],{{Rebels|Droids in Distress}} and the [[Squamatan]]s[[Poe Dameron 7|''Poe Dameron'' 7]] experienced genocide at the hands of the Empire. The [[Kubaz]] were [[Blockade of Kubindi|enslaved]] by the Empire to be turned into brainwashed [[Spy|spies]]. Even so,{{StoryCite|story=The Secrets of Long Snoot|book=From a Certain Point of View}} [[stormtrooper]] [[Sardis Ramsin|TD-7556]] distrusted the species as a whole, claiming he was not bigoted when he insulted the entire species without exception.{{StoryCite|story=Born in the Storm|book=From a Certain Point of View}} During the Age of the Empire, some human [[grav-ball]] fans on [[Lothal]] believed that alien physiognomies gave nonhuman players an unfair advantage. While there was no official league rule against nonhuman players, some individuals like [[Janus Fhurek]] did their best to exclude alien players from the grav-ball championship. In addition, the Empire also discouraged the learning of alien [[language]]s and cultures such as [[Ithorese]], and sought to impose high 'Imperial standards' for its citizens.[[Aftermath (novel)|''Aftermath'']] Growing up with the adamantly xenophobic Empire, the [[Theelin]] [[Chass na Chadic]] struggled to carve out an identity as herself without any guidance, due to seeing her heroes die fighting for freedom while left behind.{{Twitter|DelReyStarWars|status/1131561469844361217|[[Del Rey]]|quote=Chass na Chadic has watched her heroes fall one after another in the name of freedom, leaving her behind; now, as the tide turns, Chass is adrift. Alphabet's mission may be her chance to find meaning in her life—if she can be convinced not to seek it in death. #AlphabetSquadron|archiveurl=https://archive.is/XppCl}} The discriminatory policies of the Empire forced some individuals like the [[Nikto]] [[Rinnrivin Di]] to turn to a life of crime despite having the potential to become as powerful as the human [[Grand Moff]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin]]. Instead, Rinnrivin became a spice dealer who later rose to become the [[crime lord]] of a [[Rinnrivin Di's cartel|major cartel]] during the [[New Republic]] era.[[Bloodline (novel)|''Bloodline'']] The Nikto were oppressed by the Empire and its [[stormtrooper]]s. Nevertheless, some nonhumans occupied lofty roles within the Empire, such as the [[Chagrian]] [[Mas Amedda]], who served as the Empire's [[Grand Vizier]],[[Tarkin (novel)|''Tarkin'']] the [[Pau'an]] [[The Grand Inquisitor|Grand Inquisitor]], other [[Inquisitorius|Inquisitors]] such as the [[Seventh Sister]], the [[Fifth Brother]],{{Rebels|Always Two There Are}} the [[Eighth Brother]],{{Rebels|Twilight of the Apprentice}} the [[Ninth Sister]][[Darth Vader (2017) 11|''Darth Vader'' (2017) 11]] and the [[Chiss]] [[Grand Admiral]] Thrawn, who faced discrimination for his alien heritage.''[[Thrawn: Alliances]]'' Other nonhumans who worked with the Empire, either forcibly or voluntarily, were the [[Kubaz]] [[spy]] [[Garindan ezz Zavor]]{{StoryCite|story=The Secrets of Long Snoot|book=From a Certain Point of View}} and the [[Aqualish]] [[Protectorate (title)|Protectorate]] [[Gleb]] from [[Jinata Security]].''[[Star Wars Battlefront II]]'' Despite the ingrained xenophobia within the Imperial hierarchy, some Imperials like [[Moff]] [[Delian Mors]] and [[Admiral]] [[Rae Sloane]] were willing to work with some aliens like [[Nashi]] the [[Hutt]]''[[Lords of the Sith]]'' and the [[Sullustan]] crime lord [[Surat Nuat]]. Imperial law-enforcement authorities also hired alien bounty hunters like the [[Trandoshan]] [[Bossk'wassak'Cradossk|Bossk]].''[[Ezra's Gamble]]'' ====The Rebellion's response==== [[File:Liberty_for_all.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Rebel Alliance poster highlighting the plight of the Wookiees]] The Rebel Alliance's [[Declaration of Rebellion]] condemned Emperor Palpatine and his Empire for instituting a policy of blatant speciesism and genocide against the nonhuman peoples of the galaxy. Xenophobia appeared not to be limited to the Empire. The rebel [[Saw Gerrera]], who was known for his harsh methods and tactics, disliked the Geonosians due to his Clone Wars experiences and labeled them "bugs." His harsh interrogation of [[Klik-Klak]] alarmed his [[Spectres]] comrades.{{Rebels|Ghosts of Geonosis}} [[Princess]] [[Leia Skywalker Organa Solo|Leia Organa]] of [[Alderaan]] referred to the Wookiee [[Chewbacca]] as a "walking carpet" during her [[Rescue of Princess Leia|rescue]] aboard the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|Death Star]] [[battle station]]{{Film|IV}} in [[1 BBY]].{{FilmDate|IV|1|Date}} Following the [[Battle of Yavin]], the [[Alderaanian]] [[Jora Astane]] expressed disgust that the Alderaanian diaspora on the planet [[Espirion]] had intermingled with the indigenous [[Espirion (species)|Espirion]]. The mixed-blooded [[Chief]] [[Beon Beonel]] took offense and refused to have anything to do with the Alderaanian diaspora led by Leia Organa.[[Princess Leia 4|''Princess Leia'' 4]] Leia managed to repair the schism with [[R2-D2]]'s help by beaming a speech to him. As a result, Beonel helped the Alderaanian refugees defeat an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer|Imperial Star Destroyer]].[[Princess Leia 5|''Princess Leia'' 5]] After the [[Battle of Endor]], the New Republic reversed many of the Empire's policies including compensating alien species who had been enslaved by the Empire. In [[5 ABY]], [[Han Solo]], Chewbacca and their allies helped inspire a [[Liberation of Kashyyyk|Wookiee slave revolt]] on [[Kashyyyk]], which ended the Imperial subjugation of Wookiees. New Republic forces subsequently liberated more Wookiee slaves including Chewbacca's son [[Lumpawaroo]].''[[Aftermath: Empire's End]]'' During the [[Battle of Jakku]], [[Counselor to the Empire]] [[Gallius Rax]] gave a [[Gallius Rax's speech|speech]] denouncing the New Republic as a government given over to "corrosion, chaos, and the corruption born of alien minds and radical terroristic teachings." He went on to add that their New Republic opponents were subhuman and alien in every sense of the word and deserved no mercy from the Galactic Empire. ===First Order=== {{Quote|The First Order doesn't generally take on nonhumans. They say it's because the armor is made to fit only a certain body type."
"That's because nobody likes to say the word ''xenophobia'' out loud.|Former First Order Captain [[Archex]] and [[Resistance]] agent [[Vi Moradi]]|''[[Galaxy's Edge: Black Spire]]''}} By the [[Cold War]], xenophobia appeared to have resurfaced in the propaganda and labor policies of the [[First Order]], that emulated the "Old Empire." [[Propaganda]] news feeds from [[First Order High Command]] carried reports of the New Republic tolerating unchecked alien advances throughout the Outer Rim Territories. In addition, a majority of the miners involved in the First Order's [[Pressy's Tumble refinery complex|mining colony]] in [[Pressy's Tumble]] were from various alien species including [[Talz]], [[Gran]], [[Rodian]]s, [[Abednedo]], and [[Narquois]] but included a few humans.''[[Before the Awakening]]'' [[Captain]] [[Phasma]] once criticized [[Agent]] [[Terex]]'s predilection to have nonhuman servants and refused to discuss his [[Mission to Megalox Beta|mission]] until he dismissed them.[[Poe Dameron 2|''Poe Dameron'' 2]] [[General]] [[Armitage Hux]] also referred to Chewbacca as a "beast" upon [[Mission to Pasaana|capturing him]] on [[Pasaana]].{{Film|IX}} The vast majority of the forces of the [[First Order military]] were humans. They gave the reason for this as the fact that [[First Order stormtrooper armor|their armor]] was only manufactured to fit the human body. However, it was generally understood that the true reason for this was xenophobia. Nevertheless, as with the Empire, there was still at least one nonhuman who had a lofty position within the First Order: the humanoid [[Snoke]], the First Order's [[Supreme Leader]].[[Star Wars: The Force Awakens (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Awakens'' novelization]] Another alien who served the Order was the [[Symeong]] [[metalsmith]] [[Albrekh]], who worked as a [[Sith alchemist]] for the [[Knights of Ren]] when they joined the Order. Like the Empire, the First Order also associated with aliens to achieve its goals, such as the [[Quarren]] [[pirate]] [[Kragan Gorr]] and his [[Warbird gang|gang]], whom the First Order hired to attack the [[Colossus]] [[Fuel|refueling]] station.{{Resistance|The Triple Dark}} Nonhuman informants and spies, like the Twi'lek [[Marnia Un'la'na]],{{IDWAdventuresCite-2017|issue=16|story=All Aces Battle Royale (comic story)}} the [[Keteerian]] [[Teroj Kee]]{{Resistance|Dangerous Business}} and the Nikto [[Nenavakasa Nalor]]{{Resistance|The Engineer}} also worked for the Order. On [[Ikkrukk]], some [[Ikkrukkian]]s [[Ikkrukk First Order sympathizers|sympathized]] with the Order and [[Battle of Ikkrukk|battled]] against the Ikkrukkian resisters of [[Grail City]].[[Poe Dameron 30|''Poe Dameron'' 30]] ===Others=== {{Quote|Aliens are always a problem. Especially Rodians. Those little slimeballs are the scourge of the galaxy. Can't do honest business anymore without ending up with a Rodian bounty hunter on your tail.|A Kaldana Syndicate pirate, to Han, [[Qi'ra]], and [[Tsuulo]]|''[[Most Wanted]]''}} In the [[Unknown Regions]], the [[Chiss Ascendancy]] looked down on non-Chiss species, seemingly not even respecting that their intelligence could equal their own. Indeed, [[Commodore]] [[Ar'alani]] of the [[Chiss Expansionary Defense Fleet]] did not see aliens as people, comparing them to smart animals, until she visited the world of [[Solitair]], homeworld of the [[Garwian Unity]]. Faced with [[Garwian]] culture, she came to realize non-Chiss species were indeed sentient like herself, but her colleague Thrawn admitted he only viewed non-Chiss as assets. Ar'alani's respect for the personhood of non-Chiss proved vital in the [[battle over Primea]], as her insight ensured the [[Vak Combine]] joined the fight against the [[Nikardun Destiny]]. Thrawn, meanwhile, still viewed these species as assets,''[[Thrawn Ascendancy: Chaos Rising]]'' but he would come to join the Galactic Empire and form a close relationship with human [[Eli N. Vanto]]. When Vanto joined the Ascendancy, a number of Chiss resented the human initially, distrusting him until he proved his worth.''[[Thrawn: Treason]]'' The [[Kaldana Syndicate]], a criminal syndicate that operated in the [[Corellian system|Corellia system]] around [[13 BBY]], consisted solely of humans, hating anything and anyone that wasn't human. They believed that all aliens were a problem, especially Rodians, whom they considered the scourge of the galaxy. Xenophobia, however, was not the sole province of humans nor the Galactic Empire: the [[Tusken Raider|Sand People]] of [[Tatooine]] were renowned for their aggression toward outsiders''[[Star Wars: Aliens of the Galaxy]]'' and so were the [[Sephi]] of [[Isobe]].{{RebelsMagCite|39|Never Far Behind}} The carnivorous, warlike [[Ezaraa]] species also expressed xenophobic attitudes in their desire to overthrow the Empire and replace it with their own dominion where they would "consume the flesh of all the lesser species".[[The Screaming Citadel 1|''The Screaming Citadel'' 1]] Before the [[Battle of Naboo]], the [[Gungan]]s disliked outlanders, especially the [[Naboo (people)|Naboo]], though this changed after Padmé Amidala convinced [[Boss]] [[Rugor Nass]] to join forces against the [[Invasion of Naboo|Trade Federation's invasion]].{{Film|I}} ==Behind the scenes== The idea of a xenophobic Galactic Empire was explored in [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] literature and media. According to [[Jason Fry]], the [[Lucasfilm Story Group]] had worked out that the Galactic Empire in the new [[canon]] universe was not "openly or uniformly" xenophobic. However, he reasoned many staunch Imperials like the [[Star Wars Rebels: Servants of the Empire|''Servants of the Empire'']] antagonist Janus Fhurek were also committed xenophobes.{{WebCite|url=http://eleven-thirtyeight.com/2014/11/jason-fry-keeps-talking-to-us-wired-for-the-classics#more-5424|text=Jason Fry Keeps Talking To Us: Wired For The Classics|author=Mike Cooper|date=2014-11-14|work=[http://eleven-thirtyeight.com Eleven-ThirtyEight]|archivedate=20200616162735}} ''[[Thrawn (novel)|Thrawn]]'' would later depict the xenophobia of the Empire in practice; while officially Imperials were not allowed to disrespect non-humans, Imperials still would. In his production notes for [[A New Dawn (novel)|''A New Dawn'']], [[John Jackson Miller]] clarified that the Sullustan character [[Zaluna Myder]] being called a "creature" by an Imperial officer was a reference to the "species-ism" that existed within the Empire.{{Faraway|url=fiction/books/swanewdawn.html|text=Faraway Press: Star Wars: A New Dawn}} ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox| {{IncompleteList}}
